title: H & H Lawyers Website Rebuild
subTitle: Full rebuild of a four-language law-firm platform, agent-first
overviewContent: Rebuilt H & H Lawyers' website end-to-end and shipped it to production, coordinating external designers and developers. Because this was a full rebrand, I also acted as product manager through the design phase — roughly six months to align external designers and finalise the new design, followed by six months of development. The platform is a four-language (English, Korean, Japanese, Chinese) law-firm site backed by a multi-module Spring Boot backend and a React/Next.js frontend, running on AWS. I drove the build agent-first with a reusable AI pipeline embedded in the repository, and held a two-layer safety model so agent output only shipped once it passed both deterministic gates and human review.

jd: Rebuilt the firm's website end-to-end, coordinating external designers and developers and owning product, backend, frontend, and AWS infrastructure.
Acted as product manager through a six-month design phase of a full rebrand — aligning external designers on direction and finalising the design before roughly six months of development.
Built a React/Next.js/TypeScript frontend with Tailwind CSS and four-language support (English, Korean, Japanese, Chinese).

Architected a multi-module Spring Boot backend (Java 17, Gradle) with strict module boundaries and independently deployed domain API services, using QueryDSL for type-safe queries and springdoc/Swagger for API documentation.
Integrated third-party services including IP Australia and Microsoft Graph through RESTful APIs.

Deployed on AWS: containerised services on ECS Fargate (images in ECR) behind an Application Load Balancer, with RDS, S3 + CloudFront CDN, Route 53/ACM, Cognito (Azure AD SSO), Secrets Manager, and CloudWatch.
Set up CI/CD with GitHub Actions authenticating via OIDC (no long-lived keys), reusable workflows, CI quality gates, and Core Web Vitals / Lighthouse checks.

Drove development agent-first: SDLC-aligned Claude Code sub-agents (requirements → design → implementation → review → testing), skill modules, slash commands, and hook gates, with specs decomposed from GitHub Issues as the single source of truth.

Held a two-layer safety model — deterministic gates (build/test hooks) plus disciplined human review — so agent output shipped only once it passed both, feeding every correction back into CLAUDE.md / skill files as a sharpened guardrail.
Worked with non-technical stakeholders to turn requirements into shipped features, and reviewed external developers' code and designers' UI to keep quality consistent.
